text.title: Android Keystore and Signing Procedure
text.summary: End-to-end 4-step procedure to apply Android Keystore and Signing. Each step pairs typed input + bounded action + named output + decision-gate. Steps are sequential unless explicitly marked parallel.

step.name: pick-signing-mode
step.action: Decide Play App Signing (Google holds the app signing key) vs self-managed.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: provision-upload-key
step.action: Generate the upload key (or sign-the-app key for self-managed); store in the vault.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: wire-ci-signing
step.action: Inject the keystore into CI via secret store; never commit jks files.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: separate-debug-key
step.action: Ensure debug.keystore is per-developer, never committed, never reused as upload key.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

section.title: Failure handling
section.p: If a decision-gate blocks, do NOT proceed to the next step. Surface the blockage to the owner with the specific input that did not satisfy the gate. Resume only after the input is corrected.
